title: Vocabulary List 3 (Words 581-600)
description: Test your knowledge of words 581 to 600

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Cheerfully optimistic and confident about the future

options.text: Cheerfully optimistic and confident about the future
Having a reddish or pinkish color
Feeling tired and weak all the time
Very angry and ready to fight

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Complete and including everything that is necessary

options.text: Complete and including everything that is necessary
Easy to understand and simple
Very expensive and luxurious
Difficult to comprehend or understand

questions.explanation: (Verb) To confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding

options.text: To confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding
To eat away at metal through chemical action

To decorate something with colorful patterns

questions.explanation: (Verb) To persuade someone to do something by using force or threats

options.text: To persuade someone to do something by using force or threats
To work together harmoniously
To force a horse to move in circles

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective

options.text: Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective
Feeling jealous of someone else's success

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise

options.text: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
Afraid of small, enclosed spaces

questions.explanation: (Verb) To pass or go by (said of time)

options.text: To pass or go by (said of time)
To escape from a difficult situation
To collapse or fall down suddenly

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Not sufficiently strict, severe, or careful

options.text: Not sufficiently strict, severe, or careful
Very relaxed and comfortable

questions.explanation: (Noun) A person's home or permanent residence

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Acting or done without careful consideration of the possible consequences; impulsive

Having red spots on the skin from an allergy

questions.explanation: (Adjective) Occurring at irregular intervals; not continuous or steady

options.text: Occurring at irregular intervals; not continuous or steady

options.text: To remove or prevent (a need or difficulty)
To make something very obvious

options.text: A witty or clever remark

options.text: Very vivid in color, especially so as to create an unpleasantly harsh or unnatural effect; sensational

options.text: Expressed in an unclear way that is difficult to understand; not logical or well-organized

options.text: Happening by accident or chance rather than design; lucky
